channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: YouTube entrepreneur MrBeast slams Trump's 'brutal' tariffs
item.description: YouTube entrepreneur and star MrBeast slammed President Trump’s “brutal” tariffs in a post on the social platform X on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: IRS acting commissioner is resigning over deal to send immigrants' tax data to ICE, AP sources say
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service’s acting commissioner is resigning over a deal to share immigrants’ tax data with Immigration and Customs Enforcement for the purpose of identifying and deporting people illegally in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta is bringing stricter parental controls to Facebook and Messenger
item.description: Meta is bringing its Teen Accounts, which have stricter parental controls, to its Facebook and Messenger platforms on Tuesday, expanding its teen service from just Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: A Browser Vulnerability Finally Gets Addressed in Chrome 136
item.description: Google's next version of Chrome will fix a vulnerability that allowed hackers to spy on people's browsing habits, but what else has been done about it?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How Apple thinks about the 6G evolution
item.description: With its C1 5G chip and huge networking tech development division, Apple is in the networking business. So, the company is heavily enmeshed in the development of the next-generation 6G networking standard.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Data’s dark secret: Why poor quality cripples AI and growth
item.description: Data is the foundation of innovation, agility and competitive advantage in today’s digital economy. As technology and business leaders, your strategic initiatives, from AI-powered decision-making to predictive insights and personalized experiences, are all fueled by data.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: SpaceX tourists return to Earth after a private trip that orbited the poles
item.description: Four space tourists who orbited the north and south poles returned to Earth on Friday, splashing down in the Pacific to end their privately funded polar tour.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby losing popularity – Tiobe index
item.description: The Kotlin, Swift, and Ruby languages have slipped from their top 20 positions in the Tiobe index of programming language popularity. All three seem to have lost traction and to be in decline, according to Tiobe CEO Paul Jansen.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI commentary goes into full swing at Players Championship
item.description: As a golf fan, one of the events I look forward to is the Players Championship at TPC Sawgrass at the home of the PGA Tour in Ponte Vedra, Florida.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk, Navarro tangle on tariffs
item.description: Tech billionaire and close Trump ally Elon Musk tangled with the president's trade adviser Peter Navarro on tariffs over the weekend, as the administration’s new import taxes have roiled the stock market.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: World’s first AI-powered industrial super-humanoid robot
item.description: Mech the super-humanoid robot can lift up to 132 pounds and is designed to tackle stressful and repetitive tasks that often lead to workplace injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple just made it much easier to get a handle on your inbox. Here’s how
item.description: Keeping our inboxes organized often feels like an overwhelming task. If you’re fortunate, yours contains only messages from people you wish to communicate with. Realistically, though, most are cluttered with newsletters, receipts, social media digests, and more.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: DoubleClickjacking hack turns double-clicks into account takeovers
item.description: Double-clicking is something we all do, often without giving it a second thought. But it could be giving hackers permission to do something dangerous.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A cyber call to arms
item.description: Without a call to arms, the United States is dangerously vulnerable today, and still more tomorrow, to increasingly sophisticated and brazen cyberattacks. The post A cyber call to arms first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A behind-the-scenes look at Nintendo Switch 2
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Nintendo's Switch 2 is bigger and better than its predecessor of eight years past as the video game developer pushes to bring its brand into “a new era" where social experiences are at the forefront.
item.dc:creator: RYAN KRYSKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Relentless hackers abandon Windows to target your Apple ID
item.description: Experts say hackers who used to focus on Windows operating systems are increasingly targeting Apple IDs as part of a new phishing campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta is fined by Turkey after refusing to restrict content on Facebook and Instagram
item.description: Meta said it has been hit with a hefty fine for resisting Turkish government demands to limit content on Facebook and Instagram.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nintendo announces Switch 2 release date, new features in latest Direct showcase
item.description: And it's coming with a load of new features ranging from 4K, 120 frames per second support, a new LCD screen.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation
